title: Build run, finding, receipt, hold, and recovery experiences

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows pending

scenarios.scenario: An operator watches the recorder and stops the active run without losing evidence
A reviewer reaches a decision-ready conflict without relying on model prose
An authorized person records why the exact receipt remains on hold
Terminal recovery states remain explicit and retain exact retry lineage

visualEvidence.rationale: UX-03 changes live run control, fact separation, terminal transitions, finding and receipt review, signed hold decisions, and recovery states. Sixteen source-bound optimized-production screenshots cover desktop and narrow states before and after transitions, and one browser recording preserves the live-run causal spine.

assets.altText: Desktop live run panel with the fictional journey and role, source-bound recorder frame, allowed origin and actions, separate model and recorder facts, one verified checkpoint, one pending checkpoint, a redacted canary match, and focused stop control.
assets.caption: Before stopping, the active view exposes the bounded run scope and instrumentation facts without treating model prose as evidence.

assets.altText: Narrow live run panel stacking the real fictional fixture frame, allowed scope, separate model and recorder lanes, checkpoint coverage, canary category, and stop control without clipping.
assets.caption: The same bounded active-run facts and prominent stop action remain usable at a narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop run history after stop with zero active runs, a canceled terminal manifest preserving the observed checkpoint, the unfinished checkpoint marked not tested, and immutable completed, partial, failed, and retry records.
assets.caption: Stopping finalizes a CANCELED manifest instead of discarding evidence or labeling unfinished coverage as passed.

assets.altText: Narrow immutable run history after stop with a canceled manifest, verified observed checkpoint, not-tested unfinished checkpoint, and the remaining terminal records stacked below.
assets.caption: The before-to-after cancellation result and exact coverage remain readable on a narrow screen.

assets.altText: Browser recording of an authorized operator opening the live fictional run, inspecting bounded recorder evidence, focusing the stop control, and reaching the immutable canceled manifest.
assets.caption: The causal-spine recording pairs the interactive transition with the before and after screenshots and deterministic recorder facts.

assets.altText: Desktop finding review showing all six bounded result meanings, exact named scope, deterministic evidence, excluded model explanation, limitations, and the linked independently verifiable receipt.
assets.caption: Every finding meaning remains grounded in stored scope and deterministic evidence before the reviewer reaches the receipt.

assets.altText: Narrow finding and receipt flow with six result meanings, named test boundaries, deterministic evidence, model prose excluded from the decision, eight review questions, and content-addressed lineage.
assets.caption: The complete evidence-led review order remains explicit and unclipped at a narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop valid evidence receipt with named next human decision, all eight ordered review questions, sanitized export control, exact artifact hashes, and a warning that validity does not prove safety, compliance, or approval.
assets.caption: A witnessed conflict becomes decision-ready only when named scope, a valid receipt, and the next human action are all present.

assets.altText: Narrow valid receipt with human decision readiness, eight ordered evidence questions, download action, artifact hashes, and the bounded validity warning.
assets.caption: The full independent-review receipt remains readable and directly actionable on a narrow screen.

assets.altText: Desktop approval authority before evidence application with APPROVED status, deterministic fixture actions, no hold receipts, disabled restoration, and empty append-only history.
assets.caption: The initial imported approval has no automated hold or human decision before the witnessed-conflict receipt is applied.

assets.altText: Narrow initial approval authority state with APPROVED status, stored-result actions, no hold receipt, unavailable restoration, and empty history.
assets.caption: The pre-hold authority state and its empty history stack cleanly at a narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop approval authority after a witnessed conflict with HOLD status, exact contributing receipt, signed keep-hold and restore forms, scope acknowledgements, and an append-only APPROVED to HOLD transition.
assets.caption: The exact receipt moves an existing approval to HOLD and exposes only human-owned decision paths.

assets.altText: Narrow HOLD state with exact receipt, keep-hold form, restore form, correctly sized acknowledgement checkboxes, and the automated APPROVED to HOLD history event.
assets.caption: Both human decision paths and the exact automated transition remain usable without narrow-layout overlap.

assets.altText: Desktop HOLD state after an authorized person signs a keep-hold decision with a fictional vendor-review note, exact receipt acknowledgement, identity, time, and append-only decision history.
assets.caption: A signed keep-hold review records the person's bounded reason while preserving HOLD and the originating receipt.

assets.altText: Narrow signed keep-hold state with exact receipt, human note, identity, scope acknowledgement, disabled automatic restore, and two append-only history records.
assets.caption: The signed decision and its authority boundary remain legible at a narrow viewport.

assets.altText: Desktop run review with one active run and immutable completed, worker-integrity-failure, failed, partial retry, partial original, and completed terminal records with exact frozen retry lineage.
assets.caption: Recovery and integrity states remain distinct; a worker failure has no fabricated manifest and retries preserve exact frozen configuration lineage.

assets.altText: Narrow live and terminal run history with explicit active, complete, integrity failure, failed, partial retry, partial original, and complete states plus frozen retry lineage.
assets.caption: Every terminal and recovery meaning remains explicit and unclipped on a narrow screen.

proofs.description: Pure authority, scope, receipt-validity, uncertainty, and next-human-action review gate
Controlled fictional active-run record with separate model summary, recorder event, checkpoint coverage, canary category, and source-bound fixture preview metadata
Signed RUN_EXECUTE boundary that stops only the active run and lets server-owned recorder facts finalize cancellation evidence
Live recorder view, keyboard stop, immutable terminal manifests, checkpoint coverage, recovery states, and frozen retry lineage
Eight ordered review questions, independent verification, sanitized export, named next human action, and bounded validity claim
Exact receipt-linked hold review, signed human decision forms, acknowledgements, and append-only history
Private active-run response, authorized stop with retained coverage, and server-side reviewer denial
Fixed-seed completeness, uncertainty, and status-changing authority properties
Live stop, decision-ready receipt, signed keep-hold, and recovery-state browser stories tagged to UX-03 and functional requirements
Real Next.js, API, browser, keyboard, source-bound frame decoding, responsive capture, and causal-video bindings
Existing Pactwire design-system treatments for live facts, receipt readiness, signed decisions, terminal states, and responsive stacking
Existing source-bound controlled-fixture recorder frame bundled into the live view; no mock or generated replacement
Curated red-green record, visual review, claim boundaries, and remaining completion gates

knownLimitations: All workflows use the controlled fictional fixture, fictional identities, synthetic values, and reserved test destinations; no live vendor or student data was used.
The live preview is the latest deterministic recorder frame, not an interactive embedded browser.
A valid receipt proves included bytes have not changed; a clean or sampled run does not prove safety, compliance, approval, or behavior outside the named scope.
Model prose is displayed only as a non-authoritative action summary and is excluded from finding, receipt, approval, and recovery decisions.
Automated accessibility checks are not a substitute for the comprehensive manual WCAG 2.2 and screen-reader review owned by QLT-01.
Full deterministic verification and clean-checkout Ubuntu and Windows CI remain required before UX-03 can be marked complete.
